corrections.text: The article attributes 'Breakthrough Therapy Designation, Orphan-Drug Designation, and Priority Review' to '[Gilead Sciences via BioSpace]'. The BioSpace snapshot captured at review time does not contain this information; BioSpace carried the same Gilead press release syndication, which does not include the designations section. The FDA designations are factually accurate for Hepcludex but should be attributed to the Gilead Sciences/BusinessWire press release rather than BioSpace.

The article attributes specific clinical data to the European Medicines Agency — including the 48% (24 out of 50) HDV RNA clearance rate at 48 weeks versus 2% (1 out of 51) in untreated patients, and the EU conditional marketing authorisation dates of July 31, 2020 (conditional) and July 18, 2023 (standard conversion). The EMA source page timed out during automated snapshot capture and could not be verified from the local snapshot at review time. These claims are consistent with the publicly known EMA regulatory history for Hepcludex but are noted as unverified from the captured snapshot.
